{{Quote|Dr. Cinto Alaras is a designer and medical consultant at the Neuro-Saav labs on Tauber, in the Jaso sector, one of the most important medical areas in the galaxy.|A report by Rebel Task Force on Alliance Security|<ref name="AIR" />}}
'''Tauber''' was an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] that lay on the [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]]. In [[214 BBY]], an [[Bordal Contagion antidote|antidote]] to the disease known as the [[Bordal Contagion]] was created on Tauber. Two [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] later, a [[Unidentified hospital|hospital]] on Tauber was where the [[A4 laboratory assistant droid/Legends|A4 laboratory assistant droid]] designated [[EV-A4-D/Legends|EV-A4-D]] began his service, while [[Neuro-Saav Corporation]] ran material-design [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] on the world by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]. [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Cinto Alaras]] and the undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Agent/Legends|Agent]] [[Sommen]] both worked at the Neuro-Saav facilities.

==Description==
Tauber was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="FF" /> located in the [[Tauber system/Legends|Tauber system]] of the [[Jaso sector/Legends|Jaso sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> That [[Sector/Legends|sector]] lay within the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]]<ref name="EAOC" /> and was considered to be among the most important [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] areas in medicine.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[Rimma Trade Route/Legends|Rimma Trade Route]] [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] linked Tauber to the [[Polith system|Polith]] and [[Yag'Dhul system/Legends|Yag'Dhul systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Then came the Bordal Contagion of 214 BBY. I don't know what it was — some kind of plague that left people strangling on fluid in their lungs, awful stuff. I think it was the first time the Republic had thought about Rseik sector in a century or so. They figured out an antidote on Tauber and started sending holds full of it to Kabal.|Memoirs of Firmus Kett|<ref name="AuthorCut" />}}
[[File:EV-A4-D-TCW.png|thumb|right|200px|EV-A4-D began his service at a hospital on Tauber.]]
In [[214 BBY]], during the outbreak of the [[Bordal Contagion]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Rseik sector/Legends|Rseik sector]], [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Scientist/Legends|researchers]] on Tauber created an [[Bordal Contagion antidote|antidote]] to the disease. At least 6 million doses of the antidote were subsequently sent to<ref name="AuthorCut" /> the Outer Rim planet<ref name="SWR">[[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']]</ref> [[Kabal/Legends|Kabal]] for further [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] to the Rseik sector. Tauber's role in combating the disease was eventually mentioned in the memoirs of the [[Trade Defense Force]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] [[Firmus Kett]].<ref name="AuthorCut" />

Tauber was the site of a [[Unidentified hospital|hospital]]<ref name="DB" /> at some point by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].<ref name="DB date">{{TCWdates|110}} Since {{DB|droid|a4d|A-4D (Ayfour-Dee)}} establishes that [[EV-A4-D/Legends|EV-A4-D]] worked at a [[Unidentified hospital|hospital]] on Tauber prior to serving at the [[Grievous's castle/Legends|castle]] of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], as depicted in "Lair of Grievous," the [[A4 laboratory assistant droid/Legends|droid's]] [[Time/Legends|time]] on Tauber can also be placed at some point by 22 BBY.</ref> The [[Medcenter/Legends|facility]] employed [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Doctor/Legends|doctors]] as well as a staff of [[Medical droid/Legends|droids]]. The [[MerenData/Legends|MerenData]] [[A4 laboratory assistant droid/Legends|A4 laboratory assistant droid]] known as [[EV-A4-D/Legends|EV-A4-D]] began his existence as a [[EV-series supervisor droid/Legends|supervisor droid]] at the hospital. EV-A4-D dramatically improved the efficiency of the medcenter's [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. However, the doctors were disturbed by the supervisor's methods,<ref name="DB" /> and when EV-A4-D ordered the mechanical staff to operate on patients without using [[Painkiller/Legends|painkillers]], he was returned to his manufacturer.<ref name="FF" />

By [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="AIR date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was present on Tauber in the form of a local [[Imperial Prefect|prefect]]. During that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Human [[Gender/Legends|male]] Doctor [[Cinto Alaras]] worked as a designer and [[Medic/Legends|medical]] consultant at [[Neuro-Saav Corporation]] facilities on the planet, as did the undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Agent/Legends|Agent]] [[Sommen]]. The Rebel [[Task Force on Alliance Security]] noted in a report that Alaras could at some point stumble upon Sommen's true mission due to them working at adjacent stations and therefore classified Alaras' presence on Tauber as a potential threat to the Alliance.<ref name="AIR" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Dr. Alaras would not normally present a threat to the Alliance, but as Alliance Agent Sommen works in the adjacent design station, the Alliance fears Alaras may become suspicious of Sommen's activities in the Neuro-Saav offices and eventually report his suspicions to higher executives or even the local prefect.|A report by Rebel Task Force on Alliance Security|<ref name="AIR" />}}
At some point by 22 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="DB date" /> a major hospital operated on Tauber with a staff of medical droids<ref name="FF" /> as well as Human doctors.<ref name="DB" /> Twenty-four [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later,<ref name="AIR date" /> Neuro-Saav Corporation ran material-design facilities on Tauber, which included [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] and offices staffed by several designers.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tauber was first mentioned in the sourcebook ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', published in [[1995]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Tauber [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world Tauber, in grid square L-14.<ref name="TEA" />
